title: 3 Bedroom Houses for Rent in Clark-Fulton, Cleveland
metaTitle: 3 Bedroom Houses for Rent in Clark-Fulton, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Looking for a 3 bedroom house for rent in Clark-Fulton, Cleveland, near MetroHealth? See rent ranges, transit, and housing stock details, then tell us what you need.
primaryKeyword: 3 bedroom houses for rent Clark-Fulton cleveland
answerBox: Clark-Fulton is a near-west side Cleveland neighborhood along West 25th Street and Clark Avenue, next to the MetroHealth main campus. Three-bedroom houses here are mostly full two-story homes from the early 1900s; Cleveland's citywide 3-bedroom median rent is $1,350/month per Zumper's July 2026 report.

body: Clark-Fulton is a near-west side Cleveland neighborhood centered on the West 25th Street and Clark Avenue corridor, directly adjacent to the MetroHealth main campus, between Ohio City to the north and Stockyards to the south. The neighborhood's housing stock dates largely to the late 1800s and early 1900s and consists mostly of single-family homes and duplexes built close together on a dense street grid.

A 3-bedroom rental in this part of Cleveland is typically one of the larger single-family houses on the block rather than a smaller duplex unit, since older Clark-Fulton duplexes tend to split into one- and two-bedroom halves. Our current portfolio is concentrated on Cleveland's east and southeast side, with fewer homes on the near-west side where Clark-Fulton sits — [tell us your bedroom count and timeline](/p/book-showing) and our team will let you know what's available in this part of the city.

sections.h2: How much does a 3-bedroom house cost in Clark-Fulton?
body: There's no rent index specific to Clark-Fulton, so citywide Cleveland data is the closest available benchmark for a 3-bedroom home here. Zumper's July 4, 2026 report puts Cleveland's median 3-bedroom asking rent at **$1,350/month**, while RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report on professionally-managed apartment buildings shows a much higher citywide average of **$2,716** for a 3-bedroom unit — a gap that reflects newer, larger buildings rather than the older single-family housing typical of Clark-Fulton.

Our own managed portfolio of Cleveland rental homes runs roughly $700 to $1,800 a month, typically around $1,000, across single-family houses, duplexes, triplexes, and fourplexes — mostly 2- and 3-bedroom.

sections.h2: What a 3-bedroom home in Clark-Fulton typically includes
body: Most 3-bedroom houses in Clark-Fulton are late-1800s to early-1900s two-story homes with a basement and a small yard, built close to their neighbors on a tight urban grid rather than set on larger suburban-style lots. Because these are individually owned properties, finishes and included features vary — some have updated kitchens or laundry hookups, others retain more original layouts, so confirming specifics at a showing is worth the trip.
The neighborhood's proximity to MetroHealth's main campus makes it a common search for hospital staff and visiting family members who want a short commute to the medical center.

sections.h2: Transit and getting around from Clark-Fulton
body: Clark-Fulton is served by GCRTA bus routes along West 25th Street and Clark Avenue, connecting directly to downtown and to the MetroHealth main campus, per RTA's published route network. The neighborhood also sits close to I-90 and I-71, making it a practical location for drivers heading downtown or toward the west-side suburbs.

items: **Location:** Near-west side, along W. 25th St / Clark Ave, adjacent to MetroHealth's main campus
**Transit:** GCRTA bus lines along W. 25th St and Clark Ave
**Neighboring areas:** Ohio City to the north, Stockyards to the south

sections.h2: Section 8 and voucher-friendly 3-bedroom homes near Clark-Fulton
body: Every home we manage across Cleveland accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready, regardless of neighborhood or bedroom count. Our current inventory is concentrated on Cleveland's east and southeast side, with a smaller presence on the near-west side where Clark-Fulton sits, so 3-bedroom availability here varies week to week. For how CMHA vouchers work locally, see our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/), and [tell our team what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) so we can point you toward current options across the areas we serve, including our broader [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) listings.

faq.a: Clark-Fulton is a near-west side Cleveland neighborhood along the West 25th Street and Clark Avenue corridor, directly adjacent to the MetroHealth main campus, between Ohio City to the north and Stockyards to the south.

faq.q: How much does a 3-bedroom house cost to rent in Clark-Fulton?
faq.a: There's no neighborhood-specific rent index, but citywide Cleveland data from Zumper (July 2026) shows a 3-bedroom median asking rent of $1,350/month. Actual Clark-Fulton listings vary by size, age, and condition, and are generally priced well below professionally-managed apartment buildings.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage 3-bedroom homes in Clark-Fulton?
faq.a: Our portfolio is concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast neighborhoods, with fewer homes on the near-west side where Clark-Fulton is located. Availability changes regularly — tell our team what you need and we'll point you to current 3-bedroom options across the areas we serve.

faq.q: Are 3-bedroom houses in Clark-Fulton mostly single-family homes?
faq.a: Yes. A 3-bedroom rental in Clark-Fulton is typically a larger single-family house rather than a duplex unit, since the neighborhood's duplexes tend to split into smaller one- and two-bedroom halves.

faq.q: Are Section 8 vouchers accepted for 3-bedroom houses in Clark-Fulton?
faq.a: Acceptance depends on the individual landlord — Ohio has no statewide requirement, and Cleveland does not currently have a source-of-income protection ordinance. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, no matter which Cleveland neighborhood or bedroom count it involves.
